rules.name: Riparian Zone Protection
rules.description: No expansion within 100 feet of streams, rivers, or riparian transition zones
rules.regulation: Green Corridors Plan § 3.2 — Stream Buffer Requirements; NYS Environmental Conservation Law § 24-0701
rules.rationale: Riparian buffers protect water quality, bank stability, and wildlife corridors. The Hudson Highlands are water-rich; stream integrity is critical.
rules.impact_if_violated: Expansion AS PLANNED violates guardrail. Project cannot proceed without modification.
mitigation_options: Option A: Reduce expansion footprint to remain >100 ft from riparian feature
Option B: Relocate expansion to alternative area of parcel farther from stream
Option C: Implement riparian restoration plan (native plantings, erosion control) to offset impact
Option D: Propose alternative design that maintains buffer integrity
rules.approval_path: Planning Director review with environmental consultant letter. HHLT consultation recommended.

rules.name: Core Forest Preservation
rules.description: No net loss of core forest. Loss of >1 acre triggers mandatory habitat restoration or offset plan.
rules.regulation: Green Corridors Plan § 4.1 — Core Forest Threshold; NLCD Classification
rules.rationale: Core forest (unfragmented canopy >250 acres) is rare in the Hudson Highlands. Every acre lost reduces interior forest habitat and wildlife movement.
rules.impact_if_violated: Expansion affects core forest. Project requires habitat restoration or offset mitigation.
mitigation_options: Option A: Develop on-site habitat restoration plan (native plantings, invasive removal)
Option B: Fund off-site forest conservation easement or acquisition through HHLT
Option C: Reduce expansion scope to minimize forest loss (<1 acre)
Option D: Phased development with monitoring and contingency conservation
rules.approval_path: Planning Director + Conservation Board joint approval. HHLT habitat assessment required.
rules.override_authority: Planning Director with documented mitigation plan

rules.name: Bald Eagle Nesting & Foraging Habitat
rules.description: Critical species protection within 2 miles of documented bald eagle sightings or nesting areas
rules.regulation: NYS DEC Wildlife Resources § 5.0 — Protected Species; Bald and Golden Eagle Protection Act
rules.rationale: Bald eagles are recovering in the Hudson Valley. Nesting territories and foraging areas near water are sensitive to disturbance. Federal protection applies.
rules.impact_if_violated: Expansion in bald eagle habitat zone. Project requires NYSDEC consultation and seasonal construction limits.
mitigation_options: Option A: Obtain NYSDEC habitat assessment letter confirming no nesting impact
Option B: Implement seasonal construction limits (avoid nesting season: Jan-Jul)
Option C: Establish construction exclusion zones and noise limits within 1 mile
Option D: Propose compensatory habitat work (perch trees, nesting platform, shoreline restoration)
rules.approval_path: NYSDEC written habitat clearance REQUIRED. Planning Director approval after DEC clearance.

rules.name: Habitat Connectivity Corridors
rules.description: High-priority connectivity corridors identified in Green Corridors Plan must maintain wildlife movement and ecological function
rules.regulation: Green Corridors Plan § 6.0 — Corridor Integrity; Hudson Highlands biodiversity framework
rules.rationale: Wildlife corridors allow species movement, genetic diversity, and habitat access. High-priority zones are irreplaceable for landscape-scale conservation.
rules.impact_if_violated: Expansion in high-priority corridor. Project may degrade wildlife connectivity. Mitigation required.
mitigation_options: Option A: Maintain minimum 60% site permeability (unfenced, passable habitat)
Option B: Implement phased construction to maintain corridor function during development
Option C: Design landscaping to support native species and safe passage
Option D: Establish conservation easement on remaining 40% of site
rules.approval_path: HHLT consultation + Planning Director approval. May require Conservation Board review.

rules.name: Wetland Protection
rules.description: No expansion that impacts wetlands comprising >5% of site area. Wetlands are protected resources under state law.
rules.regulation: NYS Environmental Conservation Law § 24-0701 — Freshwater Wetlands Protection Act; Article 24 Regulations
rules.rationale: Wetlands provide flood control, water filtration, carbon storage, and irreplaceable wildlife habitat. Once lost, they cannot be recreated.
rules.impact_if_violated: Site contains significant wetlands. Expansion BLOCKED unless completely redesigned to avoid wetland impact.
mitigation_options: Option A: Redesign project footprint entirely to avoid all wetland areas (>5% threshold)
Option B: Pursue Article 24 permit from DEC (requires demonstrated public benefit and no feasible alternatives)
Option C: Abandon expansion; focus on non-wetland areas of parcel
Option D: Propose wetland restoration on adjacent land to offset any minor unavoidable loss
rules.approval_path: DEC Article 24 permit (state-level) + Planning Board supermajority vote (6/7). HHLT environmental review.
rules.override_authority: New York State DEC + Planning Board supermajority

decision_logic.BLOCK: Expansion cannot proceed. Rule violation is absolute unless override authority approves specific mitigation.
decision_logic.CONDITIONAL: Expansion possible with documented mitigation plan. Planning Director can approve with conditions.
decision_logic.REVIEW: Expansion requires consultation. May approve with modifications. Planning Director + external agency review.
decision_logic.CONSULT: External agency (NYSDEC, USFWS) consultation required. Approval contingent on agency clearance.

metadata.jurisdiction: Town of Philipstown, Putnam County, New York
metadata.source: Hudson Highlands Green Corridors Plan (2022) + NYS Environmental Conservation Law + Federal Protected Species Act
metadata.note: These rules represent town values as encoded in the Green Corridors Plan. Rules are enforceable by Planning Board authority. All decisions logged and auditable.
